title: Bot Pathfinding Fixes: Infinite Loop Prevention and Starvation Timer
content:
## Problem Background

In Iron Snake mode (and occasionally classic mode), AI bots would get permanently stuck in approach-and-retreat cycles: a bot paths toward food, correctly refuses to enter a tight pocket that would trap it, retreats, then deterministically re-selects the exact same losing approach and repeats forever. The game would never end.

**Root cause:** Every bot in `src/bots/*.ts` is a stateless, per-tick, greedy direction-scorer with no memory, no randomness, and no tie-breaking. Given an identical board configuration, scoring produces an identical choice, so any positional cycle is stable and never breaks. Iron Snake's irregular board masks create far more tight pockets, making this especially common there.

A secondary problem emerged after the loop guard was added: a bot that wanders productively but never reaches food could keep the game running indefinitely, requiring manual intervention.

---

## Solution 1: Loop Guard (`src/bots/loop-guard.ts`)

### Architecture Decision: Harness-Level Guard

Rather than adding memory to each of the 11 stateless bots (which would require changing the shared bot interface and rewriting every bot), a centralized guard was added at the harness level. It intercepts the chosen direction only when a loop is actually detected, leaving all bot personalities completely intact during normal play.

### Detection: Two Signals

A pure revisit counter alone proved insufficient. On a ~20-cell "approach, give up, wander back" circuit, no cell recurs 3 times until 60 moves have passed. Two signals are used together:

1. **Tight oscillation:** Any cell revisited 3+ times within a 40-move rolling window of recent head positions.
2. **Confinement ratio:** Too few distinct cells relative to total moves over the window — catches larger circuits that the revisit counter alone misses. Confirmed by simulation: the guard broke a bot out of a 20-cell ring to explore 125 cells.

### Escape Behavior

When a loop is detected, `chooseEscapeDirection` picks the legal, non-trapping move whose destination cell has been visited least recently, with random tie-breaking. The escape direction is committed for **8 ticks** (`ESCAPE_DURATION`) so the snake doesn't snap back immediately. Falls back to max `reachableArea` if no tail-reachable move exists; returns `null` if no safe move is available (harness keeps the bot's original pick).

All logic uses the existing `BotHelpers` (`simulateMove`, `analyzePosition`, `getCandidateDirections`), so it automatically respects the Iron Snake `boardMask`.

### Exported API

```typescript
type LoopMemory = { recentHeads: string[]; escapeTicks: number }
createLoopMemory(): LoopMemory
resetLoopMemory(mem: LoopMemory): void
recordHead(mem: LoopMemory, head: Position): void
isLooping(mem: LoopMemory): boolean
chooseEscapeDirection(state, helpers, mem): Direction | null
```

### Harness Wiring (`src/main.ts`)

- Per-snake memory fields: `botLoopMemory` (P1/single-player) and `botLoopMemory2` (P2), initialized with `createLoopMemory()`.
- In `chooseBotDirection` and `chooseBotDirectionForPlayer`: after the bot picks its direction, `recordHead(mem, snake[0])` is called; if `mem.escapeTicks > 0 || isLooping(mem)`, `chooseEscapeDirection` is called and its result used instead, with `mem.escapeTicks` set to `ESCAPE_DURATION`.
- `resetLoopMemory` is called at every progress point: food eaten (single- and two-player), `advanceLevel`, `repositionSnakes`, and all game start/restart paths.

### Menu Demo (`src/menu-demo.ts`)

The title-screen background animation has its own bot harness. The same `loop-guard` module is wired in there (one `LoopMemory` per demo snake), eliminating oscillation from the menu visuals without duplicating logic.

---

## Solution 2: Starvation Timer (`src/main.ts`)

### Purpose

A hard backstop that ends the game cleanly when a bot wanders without eating for too long, rather than running indefinitely.

### Budget Calculation

The starvation limit is **area-based, not length-based**: `max(100, 2 × boardArea)`. A bigger board means food can legitimately be farther away, so the limit scales with the playable area. Longer snakes do not get more time — the limit is a backstop for genuinely unreachable food, not a normal operating budget. A competent snake never approaches the limit.

A latent bug was also fixed: `expandGrid()` was doubling `gridSize` without refreshing `boardArea`, which would have made the starvation limit stale after a classic-mode grid expansion.

### Mechanics

- Per-snake move counters `movesSinceFood1` and `movesSinceFood2` increment each tick and reset to zero on eat.
- When a counter reaches the limit, the snake is declared dead via the existing `endGame()` / `endGameTwoPlayer()` paths — no new death type was introduced.
- Two-player starvation draws (both snakes starving on the same tick) are handled for free by the existing symmetry in `endGameTwoPlayer`.

### Visual Warning

- Once a snake passes **60% of its move budget** without eating, its colors blend toward an amber/red warning palette via a `lerpHex()` color interpolator in `draw()`, ramping to full warning color at the limit.
- A **pulsing overlay banner** (`#starvation-warning` in `index.html`, styled in `src/style.css`) appears the instant the hunger tint begins, naming which snake is at risk in two-player modes. It is positioned over the canvas via a `.canvas-wrap` wrapper so it does not shift the game layout. A plain urgent message was chosen over a live countdown to avoid per-tick number flicker.

### Game-Over Text

- Single-player: `"Starved!"`
- Two-player: `"Player 2 Wins! (P1 starved)"`, `"Draw — both starved!"`, etc.
- Routed through a new `deathCause` field passed to the existing game-over display logic.

---

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/bots/loop-guard.ts` | **New.** Loop detection, escape logic, `LoopMemory` type. |
| `src/main.ts` | Loop guard wiring in both bot harnesses; starvation counters, limit helper, `boardArea` fix, `deathCause` field. |
| `src/menu-demo.ts` | Loop guard wiring for demo snakes. |
| `index.html` | `#starvation-warning` banner element, `.canvas-wrap` wrapper. |
| `src/style.css` | Pulsing pill styles for starvation warning overlay. |
